finding: Required runner-sync release mirror outputs are generated after framework source delivery but are excluded from the taskflow close bundle as out-of-scope advisory dirty files, leaving a second manual governance decision before push.
expectedBehavior: When runner-sync is required by a framework task, ATM should provide a governed follow-up lane or commit bundle for release artifacts and runner-sync receipts instead of leaving them as ambiguous dirty residue.
evidenceOrRepro: ATM-GOV-0186 close dry-run/write reported release/atm-onefile and release/atm-root-drop files as excludedDirtyFiles while the runner-sync build/release had already been required for the sealed source commit.
followUp: Create a taskflow/runner-sync integration card: close should either include allowed runner output files with receipt proof or emit an explicit next command to claim and commit release artifacts safely.
